_comment: Amazon States Language (ASL) definition for the E-Commerce Lakehouse ETL pipeline.
This file is a standalone, human-readable representation of the state machine
that lives in terraform/step_functions.tf. It is NOT executed directly — Terraform
provisions the actual AWS resource. Keep this file in sync when the Terraform
Placeholder values that vary per deployment:
- JobName fields use the default name_prefix 'ecom-lakehouse-dev'.
For staging/prod, replace 'dev' with the correct environment suffix.
- TopicArn: replace '123456789012' with your AWS account ID.
Execution input shape (supplied by the aggregation Lambda):
Comment: E-commerce Lakehouse single-batch ETL pipeline — dev

RunProductsJob.Comment: Step 1: Products dimension — no upstream dependency, runs first.

RunOrdersJob.Comment: Step 2: Orders fact — no upstream dependency, runs second.

RunOrderItemsJob.Comment: Step 3: Order items — depends on products AND orders. Runs last so FK joins resolve against committed Delta tables.

AthenaValidation.Comment: Step 4: Smoke-test — confirms all three Delta tables are queryable and report row counts.

Parameters.QueryString: SELECT 'products' AS tbl, COUNT(*) AS row_count FROM ecom_lakehouse_db.products UNION ALL SELECT 'orders', COUNT(*) FROM ecom_lakehouse_db.orders UNION ALL SELECT 'order_items', COUNT(*) FROM ecom_lakehouse_db.order_items;

NotifySuccess.Comment: Step 5a: Publish success notification. $.batch is always present — never overwritten by any prior ResultPath.

Parameters.Subject: [dev] Lakehouse ETL — SUCCESS

NotifyFailure.Comment: Step 5b: Publish failure notification, then transition to terminal Fail state.

Parameters.Subject: [dev] Lakehouse ETL — FAILURE

PipelineFailed.Cause: One or more ETL stages failed. Check CloudWatch logs.
